verdict_reasoning: IZUMO4 is explicitly described as a soluble protein lacking a transmembrane domain, in contrast to IZUMO1/2/3 which are transmembrane proteins. Primary literature (Ellerman et al. 2009) classifies it as an intraacrosomal sperm protein; OMIM notes it is likely intraacrosomal; the Protein Atlas reports cytoplasmic expression in seminiferous duct cells; and the NCBI summary places it in the nucleus. There is no documented TM isoform, GPI anchor, or stable wash-resistant association with a TM partner that would anchor IZUMO4 to the outer face of the plasma membrane. No surface-directed therapeutics are known. It is a secreted/soluble intracellular protein.
